title: Supply Chain Shock
description: A major overseas supplier shuts down, causing shortages and rising prices.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: If we don’t tighten regulations, we risk a repeat of past supply chain collapses. Early intervention prevents inflation spikes.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: Intervention distorts the market. Letting prices adjust naturally preserves long-term resilience.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: Resilience doesn’t matter if businesses collapse before recovery. Short-term stability is essential.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: But heavy subsidies create dependency. We risk propping up inefficiency.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: Ignoring warning signs is what caused previous economic crashes. Prevention is cheaper than collapse.
